lede: A look at January 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Javier Pérez de Cuéllar and Janis Joplin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Megaupload
events.desc: The Hong Kong-based file-sharing website Megaupload is shut down by the FBI.

events.title: Hrant Dink
events.desc: Turkish-Armenian journalist Hrant Dink is assassinated in front of his newspaper's Istanbul office by 17-year-old Turkish ultra-nationalist Ogün Samast.

events.title: Czech Republic
events.desc: Czech Republic and Slovakia join the United Nations.

events.title: Indira Gandhi
events.desc: Indira Gandhi becomes India's first female prime minister.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: HMS Greyhound and other escorts of convoy AS-12 sink Italian submarine Neghelli with all hands 64 kilometres (40 mi) northeast of Falkonera.

events.title: American Civil Liberties Union
events.desc: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) is founded.

events.title: Giuseppe Verdi
events.desc: Giuseppe Verdi's opera Il trovatore receives its premiere performance in Rome.

events.title: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
events.desc: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe's Faust: The First Part of the Tragedy receives its premiere performance.

events.title: Batavian Republic
events.desc: The Batavian Republic is proclaimed in the Netherlands, replacing the Dutch Republic.

events.title: Hundred Years' War
events.desc: Hundred Years' War: Rouen surrenders to Henry V of England, completing his reconquest of Normandy.

births.name: Javier Pérez de Cuéllar
births.line: Peruvian politician and diplomat, 135th Prime Minister of Peru

deaths.text: Hedy Lamarr, Austrian-American actress, singer, and mathematician
Ham (chimpanzee), chimpanzee and animal astronaut, first hominid in space
Tony Garnier (architect), French architect and urban planner, designed the Stade de Gerland

observances.text: Christian feast day: Faustina and Liberata of Como
Christian feast day: Marius, Martha, Audifax, and Abachum
Christian feast day: Mark of Ephesus (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Wulfstan, Bishop of Worcester
